pages.name: Home
pages.description: Homepage for the ebook bookstore with search, featured categories, and quick access to cart and wishlist.
primary_functions: Search for ebooks using the prominent header search bar.
Navigate directly to key categories like Science Fiction, Fantasy, Romance, Business & Economics, and Self-Help.
See featured and recommended ebooks and promotions (e.g., discounts like READ10).
Access cart and wishlist via header icons from the starting point of all tasks.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Submit search form in the header or homepage hero search bar.
Click a featured category card (e.g., Science Fiction, Fantasy, Romance, Business & Economics, Self-Help) on the homepage.
Click a featured or recommended ebook card on the homepage.
Click the cart icon in the header.
Click the wishlist icon in the header.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Home' link in the header navigation.
Accessible by loading index.html directly or clicking the site logo from any page.

pages.name: Browse & Search Results
pages.description: Unified listing page for search results and category-based browsing with filters and sorting.
primary_functions: Display lists of ebooks based on a search query (e.g., 'time management', 'python programming') or a selected category (e.g., science_fiction, fantasy, romance, business_economics, self_help).

Provide sidebar filters including Format (eBook), Price range (min/max), Rating (minimum rating or sliders), Language, Availability (e.g., Available, Upcoming/Preorder), and Release Date range (e.g., start date January 1, 2025).
Offer sorting options such as Price: Low to High, Price: High to Low, Publication Date: Newest First, Customer Rating: High to Low, and Release Date: Soonest First.
Allow users to open ebook detail pages by clicking on product titles or covers, including opening multiple products in new tabs for comparison.
Support searching by author name and showing an Authors tab or section with links to individual author detail pages (e.g., 'View author page' for Neil Gaiman).
Maintain filter and sort state so users can return from product detail pages to the same filtered results (via browser back or a 'Back to results' link).

incoming_params.description: Search query string, e.g., 'time management' or 'Python programming'. When present, the page shows search results instead of a pure category listing.
Category code used for browsing, matches Category.code (e.g., 'science_fiction', 'fantasy', 'romance', 'business_economics', 'self_help', 'all_ebooks').
Active tab on the browse page, e.g., 'products' (default) or 'authors'. When set to 'authors', searchAuthors is used and author results are shown.
Current results page for pagination (1-based index).
Optional page size parameter used for pagination. Defaults to a standard size when omitted.
Sort option identifier, matching getBrowseSortOptions.id (e.g., 'price_asc', 'price_desc', 'publication_date_desc', 'rating_desc', 'release_date_asc').
Minimum price filter value in USD, e.g., 5 for $5. Omitted when no lower bound is applied.
Maximum price filter value in USD, e.g., 15 for $15. Used in tasks that constrain price ranges.
Minimum average rating filter value (0–5), e.g., 4.0, 4.2, or 4.5.
Optional rating preset identifier, matching getBrowseFilterOptions.ratingPresets.id (e.g., 'stars_4_up', 'stars_4_5_up'). Used for quick rating filter shortcuts.
Language code for Language filter, matching getBrowseFilterOptions.languages.code (e.g., 'english', 'spanish').
Availability filter code, matching getBrowseFilterOptions.availabilityOptions.code (e.g., 'available', 'upcoming', 'preorder'). Used for 'Upcoming'/'Preorder' fantasy ebooks.
Release date range start (ISO date string, e.g., '2025-01-01') used when filtering upcoming/preorder titles after a certain date.
Optional release date range end (ISO date string). Used to cap the release window if needed.
Format filter, currently 'ebook'. Included so the UI can explicitly filter for ebooks, matching Product.format.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a product title or cover in the results grid/list. The current browse URL (including filters and sorting) is preserved as returnUrl for the 'Back to results' link.
From the Authors tab or an author result, click the author name or 'View author page' link.
Change filters, sorting, or paginate within the browse/search results.

access_methods.description: Accessible via header category links such as 'Science Fiction', 'Fantasy', 'Romance', 'Business & Economics', 'Self-Help', and 'All eBooks', which point to browse.html with categoryId set.
Accessible via the 'Browse all eBooks' footer link, which points to browse.html?categoryId=all_ebooks.
Accessible when redirected from the homepage search form or other in-page links, with query and/or categoryId parameters.

pages.name: Product Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a single ebook with full information and purchase/wishlist actions.
primary_functions: Show complete ebook information including title, author, description, price, rating, language, format, page count, publication date, and release date (for upcoming/preorder titles).
Provide primary purchase actions: 'Add to cart' for available ebooks and 'Preorder eBook' for upcoming titles that support preorders.
Allow users to add or remove the ebook from their wishlist with an 'Add to wishlist' (or 'Remove from wishlist') button.
Enable users to compare details like page count between multiple open product pages to decide which ebook to purchase.
Display a 'Customers also bought' or 'You may also like' recommendations section with ebook cards that show price and rating, linking to other product detail pages.
Include a 'Back to results' link that returns the user to the last browse/search results page with filters and sorting preserved.

incoming_params.description: Product.id of the ebook to display. Required for loading product details, price, rating, language, page count, etc.
Optional encoded URL pointing back to the originating browse or author listing page. Used for the 'Back to results' link when present.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'View cart' or equivalent link after adding an ebook or preorder to the cart.
Click a 'View wishlist' link after adding the ebook to the wishlist.
Click a product card in the 'Customers also bought' / 'You may also like' recommendations section.
Click the 'Back to results' link when returnUrl refers to a previous browse.html listing. Parameters are reconstructed from returnUrl.

access_methods.description: Accessed by navigating to product.html?id={productId} from listings, wishlist, cart, or external links.
Can be bookmarked or shared as a deep link to a specific ebook.

pages.name: Cart
pages.description: Shopping cart page to review, edit, and prepare ebook purchases before checkout.
primary_functions: List all ebooks currently in the cart, including preorders, with title, price, quantity (if applicable), and subtotal per item.
Allow users to remove items from the cart or adjust quantities where relevant.
Show order summary with subtotals, applied discounts, estimated taxes (if applicable), and total amount.
Provide a promo code field where users can enter and apply codes such as 'READ10' and immediately see updated pricing.
Offer a 'Proceed to checkout' button to go from the cart to the checkout page.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'Proceed to checkout' button after reviewing cart items and promo code.
Click on a product title or cover from the cart item list to review its details.
Click a 'Continue shopping' link from the cart page.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Cart' icon/link in the header navigation.
Accessible from mini-cart overlays or after adding items from product pages.

pages.name: Checkout
pages.description: Final purchase step to review order details and enter payment and purchaser information.
primary_functions: Display a concise order summary including all ebooks and preorders, quantities, prices, discounts, and total cost.
Collect purchaser details such as name and email required for ebook delivery and receipts (without requiring account creation).
Collect payment details and allow the user to confirm payment for the order, including preordered fantasy ebooks.
Submit the order and display an on-page confirmation state/message indicating that the purchase or preorder has been successfully placed.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Back to cart' link/button from the checkout page.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Proceed to checkout' button on cart.html.

pages.name: Wishlist
pages.description: Page for viewing and managing ebooks saved for later.
primary_functions: Display all ebooks that have been added to the wishlist, including business, self-help, and any other genres.
Allow users to remove ebooks from the wishlist.
Allow users to move or add wishlist items to the cart directly to facilitate later purchase.
Link back to product detail pages for any wishlist item to review full information before purchasing.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a wishlist item's title or cover to view full product details.
Click a 'View cart' link after moving items from wishlist to cart.
Click 'Continue browsing' from the wishlist page.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Wishlist' icon/link in the header navigation.
Accessible from 'Add to wishlist' confirmations on product pages.

pages.name: Author Detail
pages.description: Dedicated page for a specific author with their bio and ebooks.
primary_functions: Show author information such as name, photo, and brief biography.
List the author’s ebooks with the ability to filter by Format (e.g., eBook only) and sort by Customer Rating, Publication Date, or Price.
Allow users to select an ebook (e.g., the first Neil Gaiman ebook under $18) and navigate to its product detail page.
Act as the landing page when users click 'View author page' from search results or other site areas.

incoming_params.description: Author.id for the author whose page should be displayed.
Current page of author ebook listings for pagination.
Optional page size for author ebook listings.
Sort option for the author's ebooks, matching getBrowseSortOptions.id (e.g., 'rating_desc', 'publication_date_desc', 'price_asc').
Optional minimum price filter when browsing an author's ebooks.
Optional maximum price filter when browsing an author's ebooks (e.g., under $18).
Optional minimum rating filter for author ebooks.
Format filter, typically 'ebook', used to restrict listings to ebooks.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on an ebook in the author's book list.
Change sort, filters, or paginate within the author ebook list.

access_methods.description: Accessed as author.html?id={authorId} from the browse Authors tab or from an author link on product pages.
Can be bookmarked or shared as a deep link to an author's page.

pages.name: About
pages.description: Information about the ebook bookstore and its mission.
primary_functions: Explain the purpose, vision, and unique aspects of the ebook bookstore.
Provide high-level information about content coverage (genres, languages, formats).
Offer links or references to Help/FAQ and Contact pages for users needing more details about using the site.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a link such as 'Learn how to search and buy ebooks' from the About page.
Click a 'Contact us' or 'Get in touch' link from the About page.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'About' link in the footer navigation.
Can be accessed directly via about.html or from external references.

pages.name: Help & FAQ
pages.description: Support and guidance for using the ebook store, including purchases, preorders, and downloads.
primary_functions: Answer common questions about searching, filtering, sorting, purchasing, preordering, and downloading ebooks.
Explain how to use the cart, apply promo codes, and manage the wishlist.
Provide troubleshooting tips for issues like missing downloads or preorder availability.
Link users to the Contact page if they need further assistance.

incoming_params.description: Optional FAQ category to pre-filter questions (e.g., 'search_filter', 'purchasing', 'preorders', 'downloads', 'promo_codes', 'wishlist').

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Contact support' or similar link after reading FAQs.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Help & FAQ' link in the footer navigation.
May be linked from error messages, support prompts, or other informational sections on the site.

pages.name: Contact
pages.description: Contact page for support or general inquiries.
primary_functions: Provide a simple contact form for users to send support requests or questions.
Display contact details such as support email address and, if applicable, business address or support hours.
Offer links back to Help/FAQ for self-service help before contacting support.

incoming_params.description: Optional pre-filled subject line for the contact form, e.g., 'Question about preorders' from an FAQ link.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Submit the contact form; on success, the page remains contact.html but shows a confirmation message.
Click a 'View FAQs' link from the contact page.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Contact' link in the footer navigation.
Form submissions stay on contact.html and show success or error states using submitContactRequest.

pages.name: Privacy Policy
pages.description: Legal page describing how user data and privacy are handled.
primary_functions: Inform users about what personal data is collected and how it is used.
Explain data storage, security, and sharing practices relevant to ebook purchases and communication.
Describe user rights related to their data and how to exercise those rights.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Privacy Policy' link in the footer navigation.
Can be accessed directly (e.g., from emails or external privacy references).

pages.name: Terms & Conditions
pages.description: Legal terms governing use of the ebook store and purchases.
primary_functions: Define the conditions under which users may access and use the site and its content.
Outline the terms related to ebook purchases, licensing, and acceptable use.
Clarify limitations of liability and other standard contractual clauses.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a link from Terms & Conditions referencing the Privacy Policy.
Click a link from Terms & Conditions referencing the Refunds & Digital Goods Policy.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Terms & Conditions' link in the footer navigation.
May be linked from the checkout agreement text or other legal references.

pages.name: Refunds & Digital Goods Policy
pages.description: Policies related to refunds, cancellations, and digital ebook delivery.
primary_functions: Explain refund and cancellation rules for ebook purchases and preorders.
Clarify how and when users receive access to purchased or preordered ebooks.
Describe any exceptions, such as non-refundable items or time limits for refund requests.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Need more help?' link on the Refunds & Digital Goods Policy page.
Click a 'Contact support about a refund' link.

access_methods.description: Accessible via the 'Refunds & Digital Goods Policy' link in the footer navigation.
May be linked from order confirmation emails or download issues pages.

header_links.description: Homepage with search and featured ebooks
View cart and manage items before checkout

footer_links.description: Get help using the site and find answers to common questions
Review terms for using the site and purchasing ebooks
Understand refund and digital delivery policies
View the full catalog of ebooks
